threats.title: Drought and Water Shortage
threats.description: Regardless of geographic location, periods of reduced rainfall are expected. For non-agricultural industries, drought may not be impactful to operations until it reaches water rationing.

threats.title: Earthquakes
threats.description: Earthquakes are sudden shaking events caused by movement under the earth's surface. They usually last less than one minute but can have widespread and long-lasting impact.

threats.title: Fire and Wildfires
threats.description: Regardless of location or building material, fire is a concern for every business and can result in total loss of technology hardware, backups, and paper records.

threats.title: Floods
threats.description: Flooding is the most common natural hazard and requires understanding local floodplains and event frequency. Location of critical technologies should be considered.

threats.title: Hurricanes and Tropical Storms
threats.description: Hurricanes and tropical storms are powerful natural disasters with high winds, flooding, and infrastructure damage that can significantly disrupt operations.

threats.title: Landslides and Debris Flow
threats.description: Landslides can be triggered by earthquakes, storms, volcanic eruptions, fire, and human land modification. They can occur quickly with little notice.

threats.title: Pandemic (Disease) Outbreaks
threats.description: Due to a wide range of scenarios, consideration should be given to both likely magnitude and business actions that can lessen operational impacts of pandemics.

threats.title: Severe Weather
threats.description: Severe weather is a broad category of meteorological events ranging from damaging winds to hail.

threats.title: Space Weather
threats.description: Space weather includes natural events in space that affect near-earth environment and satellites, most commonly associated with solar flares.

threats.title: Thunderstorms and Lightning
threats.description: Thunderstorms can occur year-round and are associated with hazardous events including flash flooding. Lightning can cause equipment damage, fires, and fatalities.

threats.title: Tornadoes
threats.description: Tornadoes occur globally and can happen any time of year. Strong tornadoes can destroy all but the best-built structures.

threats.title: Tsunamis
threats.description: All tsunamis are potentially dangerous even when not every coastline is damaged. They can strike along most coastlines and cause severe impacts.

threats.title: Volcanoes
threats.description: Volcanoes are geographically fixed, but fallout can create downwind impacts for thousands of miles, affecting transportation and electrical grids.

threats.title: Winter Storms and Extreme Cold
threats.description: Winter storms include ice storms, heavy snowfall, and extreme cold. They can significantly impact operations and transportation over wide areas.

threats.title: Civil or Political Unrest
threats.description: Civil or political unrest can be singular or widespread events that are unexpected and unpredictable and can occur anywhere at any time.

threats.title: Technology and Cybersecurity
threats.description: Cyber incidents are often detected after occurrence and can have broad effects across individual, organizational, geographic, and national levels. This includes ICT vulnerabilities in software and hardware impacting confidentiality, integrity, availability, and safety.

threats.title: Hazardous Materials Emergencies
threats.description: Hazardous materials emergencies include accidental industrial incidents ranging from chemical spills to groundwater contamination.

threats.title: Nuclear, Biological and Chemical (NBC) Weapons
threats.description: Use of NBC weapons is a consideration, including dirty bomb scenarios that scatter radioactive, chemical, or biological material over wide areas.

threats.title: Physical Crime
threats.description: Physical crime includes crimes of opportunity such as theft, vandalism, riots, looting, arson, and related criminal activity.

threats.title: Terrorism and Armed Attacks
threats.description: Armed attacks can range from single actors to coordinated assaults and involve blade weapons, blunt objects, firearms, or explosives.

threats.title: Utility Service Disruption
threats.description: Sustained loss of electricity, internet, natural gas, water, or sanitation can directly impact operations and service delivery.

threats.title: Dysfunctional Management Practices
threats.description: Weak, ineffective, or incompetent management can increase risk by failing to make and support risk-based decisions, causing absent or deficient controls.

threats.title: Human Error
threats.description: Human error includes non-malicious and unpredictable actions such as misconfigurations, misunderstandings, and other unintentional accidents.

threats.title: Technical / Mechanical Failure
threats.description: Non-malicious failure due to defects in technology, materials, or workmanship can cause malfunctions, reliability concerns, or catastrophic damage.

threats.title: Statutory / Regulatory / Contractual Obligation
threats.description: Laws, regulations, and contractual obligations can weaken controls or materially change cost, competitiveness, and supply-chain exposure.

threats.title: Redundant, Obsolete/Outdated, Toxic, or Trivial (ROTT) Data
threats.description: ROTT data is untrustworthy information used in business processes despite issues with currency, accuracy, integrity, or applicability.

threats.title: Artificial Intelligence and Autonomous Technologies (AAT)
threats.description: AAT failures can result from hardware issues, bias, algorithmic flaws, emergent properties, and unintended consequences.

threats.title: Fraud, Corruption, and/or Willful Criminal Conduct
threats.description: Willful criminal conduct includes fraud, corruption, theft, and illegal content with intent, knowledge, recklessness, or negligence.

threats.title: Conflict of Interest (COI)
threats.description: COI exists when concerns/goals of parties are incompatible or when decision-makers can derive personal benefit from official actions.

threats.title: Macroeconomics
threats.description: Macroeconomic factors can disrupt supply chains and operations through recession, sanctions, demand shocks, inflation, unemployment, and price volatility.

threats.title: Foreign Ownership, Control, or Influence (FOCI)
threats.description: FOCI pertains to foreign ability to influence management or operations, potentially enabling unauthorized access or adverse contract performance.

threats.title: Geopolitical
threats.description: Geopolitical dynamics can affect supply chains through political intervention, causing disruption, cost increases, and investment impacts.

threats.title: Sanctions
threats.description: Sanctions risk pertains to suspension, exclusion, or other enforcement actions on third parties that can materially affect supply chain continuity.

threats.title: Counterfeit / Non-Conforming Products
threats.description: Counterfeit or non-conforming products can cause safety, reliability, maintenance, and performance risks due to poor integrity and quality.

threats.title: Operational Environment
threats.description: Operational environment threat pertains to hazardous place-of-performance conditions that expose organizations to operational and financial risk.

threats.title: Manufacturing and Supply
threats.description: Manufacturing and supply threats include capacity constraints, sole-source concentration, obsolescence, and material delivery disruptions.

threats.title: Product Quality and Design
threats.description: Inherent design and quality problems in materials, production, logistics, or packaging can cause products/services to fail standards and specifications.

threats.title: Financial
threats.description: Financial distress can lead to inability to meet obligations, hostile takeover exposure, and bankruptcy-related interruptions.

threats.title: Human Capital
threats.description: Human capital threats involve skills, knowledge, and labor conditions that can disrupt market ability to produce goods/services and meet demand.

threats.title: Transportation and Distribution
threats.description: Transportation/logistics disruptions can cause shipment delays, supply chain breaks, higher costs, and reputational damage.

threats.title: Infrastructure
threats.description: Infrastructure threats involve availability and resilience of fundamental facilities/systems such as buildings, transport networks, utilities, and equipment.
